# Transcrição: 1-10Story

- **Fonte:** 1-10Story
- **Duração:** 10:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:03]

So I remember like it was yesterday. This was back in the mid 90s and I got this email in. And this is back when you didn't get that many emails. And this email was different from any email I had ever received. It was really, really long. It was crazy. The subject line was turn your computer into a money machine. And I looked at this email because back in those days, you looked at every email that came into your inbox. And it was this really long email. In fact, it was so long that after I started reading it and it started to fascinate me and pull me in, I actually printed it out. And the printers back then were really slow and it printed page after page after page. I can't remember how many pages long it was, but it was probably eight or 10 pages long. And I read this. And it was all about this idea of publishing information online and selling that information and building a business. And that name, turn your computer into a money machine. Well, it sounds incredibly cheesy right now. But it even sounded cheesy back then. But as I read this and I read over and over and I printed it out and I read the entire thing. And then the next day I read the entire thing, it just started to make sense to me. Because I could see, I knew that the Wall Street Journal had been published for over a hundred years at that time. And so I could see that there was money in publishing. I could see that publishing was a real business. And it just seemed to me to make sense that publishing online could be a business. Now I'd never sold anything ever in my life. I'd certainly never sold anything that I created. I'd never had a business before, but it just made so much sense. So day after day, every day before I went to bed, I would read the entire letter and I would think it through. And then finally I made the decision. I wrote out a check and I sent it off for $99.50. I sent this off to get this product. It came on a three and a half inch floppy disk. And the amazing thing is that all of my success, everything I've built since then, all started from that one email and that one decision to buy this product called Turn Your Computer into a Money Machine. And that is, that's what gave me the start. Now, the reason I tell you that story isn't so much really to communicate anything. It was just to illustrate how a story can pull you in. Hopefully I did an okay job telling that story and you became interested about what is this email about? What could Turn Your Computer into a Money Machine mean? These letters mean a lot to me because of what that product did for me over the years. It ended up being a good product. But one of the things that I've learned over the years

## [03:03 - 06:04]

is just how powerful stories are. And the whole idea of product launch formula is based around creating a story with your marketing. Turn your marketing into an event and one of the easiest ways to do that is with a story. So stories are embedded throughout my marketing and I think you should do the same. And part of what I'm gonna teach you in PLF is how to do that. Because first I just wanna go over, I'll show you how and in the next video we'll really go deep into how to do it within a launch. But I just wanna talk about some of the things that stories give you. So first of all, stories are super engaging in this current environment that we live in which we all are way more distracted than we ever have been in the entire history of humanity because we're walking around with these phones that are built in distraction machines. We're sitting in front of our computers which are built in, they're just incredible distraction machines. So getting and holding someone's attention is really hard. And one of the easiest ways to do that is with stories because we're programmed for eons to be story machines. Story telling machines and story listening machines. So stories make you way more engaging. They're way more sticky. This is the way you get people to stick and listen to your entire content and come back for your content and for your marketing. Stories are really good at revealing yourself. They're how people learn about you. And when you're selling within your launches, make no mistake, they're buying the product but they're also buying you. They have to buy you and they have to believe in you and they have to relate to you before they can think about buying your product. So it's in revealing yourself and revealing your values in establishing and communicating your values. That's how people get to know you. So stories are great for revealing values. Like even in that story, when I talked about $99.50, hopefully just the way I said that conveyed that I started from I wasn't wealthy. I understand the value of a dollar that spending money means something to me. Stories are also great at establishing your authority. So instead of saying like, I'm amazing, I'm awesome, I'm great, people look at that as bragging. But if you tell a story, you can establish your authority without looking like you're bragging. Like for instance, right at the beginning of the story, I said this was from the 90s. So in the 90s is when I learned to market. It's when I learned online marketing, when I established my first online business. So that was a little authority piece built into the beginning of that story.

## [06:04 - 09:05]

It wasn't like I had to pound my chest and say, I've been doing this forever, I'm amazing, I'm fantastic. It's just by dropping that little line that this is from the 90s that establishes that I've been at this for a very long time. There's built-in authority there. Stories are the way that you teach without being boring. And if you think about it, I mean like what did I teach in that story? There wasn't a ton of teaching, but I taught that the way I got to where I am was by buying a product that taught me about online business. So if you think about it, I'm selling products about that that are training for online business. So just that little subtle thing that was in there that, well, I'm a student first and I learned, and this is how I learned, was from a digital product, not a bad thing to do. Pre-sell, I really didn't do any pre-selling in that story, but in your stories, you can start to pre-sell and establish the value of what you're going to be selling. Another big one is to answer objections. So I could tell stories about, again, that story didn't answer objections, but you can tell stories that raise whatever the objections are in your market and answer them. I guess if you think about it, one of the objections that people considering Proclete Formula would be, would be like, wow, I'm not a marketer. I don't know how to sell. I've never sold anything. And I talked about all of those things and about how this training helped me overcome that. So yeah, it's sort of interesting. Another one is connection. And this is along the lines of being engaging and revealing you and revealing your values. But if you get good at telling stories, then the viewer or the person listening to your story can start to place themselves within your story. And then another one is just being likable. And so a key to, it's that no like and trust. People have to buy you before they buy your product. And often you can come across, it's very like, I hope you are likable and I hope you do likable things and that you're nice to people and you're kind to people. And one of the ways to establish being likable is through your stories. So in that story, you know, it's definitely a story of a very humble beginning. ## [09:05 - 10:15]

we're gonna be talking about launch stories and launch themes. So here's your PLC one, two, three, and there's your sales video, right? So this is super conceptual right here. We'll drill down on it, but you really want an overall story arc. So you begin here and actually the way the story arc works is more like this, it's the rising action. That's what we learned back in high school. And then it's the falling action is when they make your purchase. But it's one big story arc, but then if we look within, say, let's, this is PLC one, you'll also have a story arc over each video. So you'll have this story arc here, a story arc here, a story arc here. And you'll actually have even a story arc in between those. That's that PS path that we talk about. So you definitely are full on using stories throughout your launch. They make your launch a lot more compelling, a lot more interesting, and it gets people coming back for each piece of pre-launch content. So absolutely use stories, practice stories, get good at stories, so they'll make a huge difference in your launch and your entire business.
